title: selago lamprocarpa
intro: Selago lamprocarpa, also known as "Cape Midge", is a flowering plant native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and it is also used to treat various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Selago lamprocarpa is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be applied topically as a poultice or cream.
